flashcards.back: A framework proposed by ANSI/SPARC in 1975 that separates database into three levels: external, conceptual, and internal to achieve data independence.
The user view level - describes how end users see the data, specific to each application or user group.
The logical level - describes the entire database structure for all users, independent of any application.
The physical level - describes how data is physically stored and accessed, including file structures and indexing.
The ability to change schema at one level without affecting the schema at the next higher level.
Changes to the conceptual schema without affecting external schemas or application programs.
Changes to the internal schema without affecting the conceptual schema.
ANSI (American National Standards Institute) through its SPARC (Standards Planning and Requirements Committee) in 1975.

flashcards.front: What is the main purpose of three-schema architecture?
flashcards.back: To achieve data independence, allowing changes in schema at one level without affecting higher levels.

flashcards.front: What is a schema in DBMS?
flashcards.back: A description or definition of the database structure, including relationships between data elements.

flashcards.front: What is mapping in three-schema architecture?
flashcards.back: The process of transforming data between schema levels - external/conceptual and conceptual/internal.
It provides data abstraction, reduces application-database coupling, and simplifies database modification and maintenance.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between external and conceptual schema?
flashcards.back: External schema is user-specific views; conceptual schema is the unified global view of the entire database.

flashcards.front: Which schema level is closest to physical storage?
flashcards.back: Internal schema - it describes the physical storage details like file organization, indexing, and access paths.

flashcards.front: Which data independence is harder to achieve and why?
flashcards.back: Logical data independence is harder because it requires restructuring the conceptual schema without affecting user views.
